beats.prompt: Quick recall. XOR is the secret weapon here вҖ” you met it in Game Theory as the nim-sum. Match each XOR fact to its value.

pairs.left: a вҠ• a for any a

feedback.correct: Nim-sum is just XOR вҖ” per-bit, no-carry addition. 3вҠ•4вҠ•5 = 011вҠ•100вҠ•101 = 010 = 2. And xвҠ•x = 0 because every bit cancels itself. This cancellation is exactly how we'll find the lone number.
hints: XOR adds each bit column independently with no carry вҖ” 1+1 = 0, not 10.
3=011, 4=100, 5=101; XOR column by column: 0вҠ•1вҠ•1=0, 1вҠ•0вҠ•0=1, 1вҠ•0вҠ•1=0 вҶ’ 010 = 2. And any xвҠ•x=0.
Match: nim-sum{3,4,5} вҶ’ 2; aвҠ•a вҶ’ 0.

beats.prompt: Bet: in the list [4, 1, 2, 1, 2] every number appears twice except one. What is the fastest way to find the lone number?

Sort first.note: Worth testing вҖ” XOR-all is one pass with no sorting. Pairs cancel under XOR (xвҠ•x=0), so the survivor is the answer directly.

Count each.note: Worth testing вҖ” you don't need a tally at all. Equal values XOR to 0; unequal values survive. One loop, zero extra memory.

XOR them (4).note: Right вҖ” pairs cancel under XOR; 4 survives. 4вҠ•1вҠ•2вҠ•1вҠ•2 = 4. One pass, no data structure needed.

beats.prompt: Before the tricks: one idea that makes all of them click.

interaction.title: Bitwise = every bit, independently, at once
interaction.body: AND, OR, XOR, and shift don't treat the whole number as one blob. They act on each bit column in parallel вҖ” bit 0 is independent of bit 1, bit 1 of bit 2, and so on. That's why x&(xвҲ’1) can surgically clear just the lowest 1-bit, and why XOR can cancel pairs simultaneously.

feedback.correct: Keep this in mind: bit ops work column by column, all columns at once. Each trick exploits one column's independence from the rest.
hints: Think of each bit position as its own mini-circuit: AND/OR/XOR/shift run on all of them simultaneously.
That column-independence is why x&(xвҲ’1) clears exactly one bit, and why xвҠ•x=0 cancels every bit.
Read it, then tap Continue.

beats.prompt: Guaranteed early win. Is 16 a power of 2? Use x & (xвҲ’1) to decide вҖ” type true or false.

feedback.correct: True вҖ” 16 & 15 = 10000 & 01111 = 0, so 16 is a power of 2. x&(xвҲ’1) clears the lowest set bit; a power of 2 has exactly one set bit, so the result is 0.
hints: Compute x & (xвҲ’1): if the result is 0 (and x > 0), x is a power of 2.
16 in binary is 10000; 15 is 01111. They share no 1-bit, so AND = 0 вҮ’ power of 2.
The answer is true.

beats.prompt: Watch x & (xвҲ’1) in action. Start with 12 = 1100вӮӮ and tap Apply repeatedly вҖ” the lowest 1-bit clears each time. How many steps to reach 0?

feedback.correct: 12 вҶ’ 8 (step 1), 8 вҶ’ 0 (step 2): 2 set bits in 12 = 1100вӮӮ. Counting steps to 0 is how x&(xвҲ’1) computes popcount вҖ” one step per set bit.
hints: xвҲ’1 flips the lowest 1-bit and all the zeros below it, then AND keeps only the higher 1-bits.
12=1100: 12&11=1000=8 (lowest 1 cleared); 8&7=0 (only set bit cleared). Two steps.
Each step to 0 counts one set bit вҖ” the step count is the popcount.

beats.interviewNote: These exact one-liners вҖ” power-of-2 test, multiply-by-shift, XOR-to-find-the-loner вҖ” are direct Green Book В§7.2 interview questions. They're fast because they operate on all bits at once.
beats.prompt: Shifts multiply. Here x=6 is left-shifted by k=3 positions: each bit moves left, so the value doubles per step, giving 6 Г— 2Ві = 48. The side panel shows (x<<3) вҲ’ x = 8x вҲ’ x = 7x, multiplying by 7 with no Г— operator.

feedback.correct: x<<3 = xВ·2Ві = 8x. Subtract the original: 8xвҲ’x = 7x. For x=6: 48вҲ’6=42. Shifting is free multiplication вҖ” and (x<<3)вҲ’x is the Green Book's trick for multiplying by 7.
hints: Left-shifting by k positions appends k zero bits on the right, which multiplies the value by 2бөҸ.
So x<<3 = 8x. Then 8xвҲ’x = 7x вҖ” multiply by 7 without a * operator.

beats.prompt: Apply it. How many set bits (1-bits) does 11 have? Use the repeated x&(xвҲ’1) trick to count.

feedback.correct: 11 = 1011вӮӮ вҖ” three 1-bits. Via x&(xвҲ’1): 11вҶ’10вҶ’8вҶ’0, three steps. Each step clears one set bit.
hints: Repeatedly clear the lowest 1-bit with n & (nвҲ’1); count the steps until you reach 0.
The answer is 3.

beats.prompt: Held-out transfer. Every number appears twice in [7, 3, 5, 3, 7] except one. XOR everything together to find the lone number.

feedback.correct: XOR cancels the pairs; 5 survives. 7вҠ•3вҠ•5вҠ•3вҠ•7: the 7s cancel, the 3s cancel, leaving 5.
hints: XOR everything together: equal values cancel (xвҠ•x=0), the unique value remains.
The answer is 5.

beats.prompt: Prove you've got it. Every number in [4, 1, 2, 1, 2] appears twice except one. Find the lone number by XOR-ing everything together.

interaction.scenario: Given the array [4, 1, 2, 1, 2]: every element appears exactly twice except one. XOR all elements together вҖ” equal values cancel, and the survivor is your answer.

feedback.correct: XOR-all: 4вҠ•1вҠ•2вҠ•1вҠ•2 = (1вҠ•1)вҠ•(2вҠ•2)вҠ•4 = 0вҠ•0вҠ•4 = 4. The pairs cancel, the loner survives вҖ” one pass, no extra memory.
hints: XOR every element: equal values cancel to 0 (xвҠ•x=0), the unique element remains.
4вҠ•1вҠ•2вҠ•1вҠ•2: the two 1s cancel, the two 2s cancel, leaving 4.
The answer is 4.

feedback.correct: Bit tricks exploit the per-bit independence of bitwise ops. x&(xвҲ’1) clears the lowest set bit вҖ” so it's a power-of-2 test and a popcount engine. x<<k multiplies by 2бөҸ, giving tricks like (x<<3)вҲ’x = 7x. And XOR is carry-free parity: pairs cancel, the unique element survives. Next: Encoding the Answer вҖ” mixed review choosing the right encoding.
hints: x&(xвҲ’1): clears the lowest set bit вҮ’ power-of-2 test; count steps to 0 вҮ’ popcount.
x<<k: multiplies by 2бөҸ. Combine shifts to multiply without *.
XOR: carry-free parity. Every pair cancels; the lone value survives.
